title: Related if/else if statements should avoid having the same condition
description: Related "if/else if" statements should not have the same conditionAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugunusedpitfallWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoA chain ofif/else ifstatements is evaluated from top to bottom. At most, only one branch will be executed: the first statement with a condition that evaluates totrue. Therefore, duplicating a condition leads to unreachable code inside the duplicated condition block.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

conditions: Same condition appears in multiple branches of the chain
Conditions are exactly identical (not just similar)

exclusion_conditions: Different operators or values in conditions
never_trigger_on: Nested if statements (not part of else if chain)
logic.reasoning: Detects if/else if chains where the same condition appears multiple times, making subsequent branches with duplicate conditions unreachable. Uses regex to match the exact condition text between if statements in an else if chain. This identifies dead code that can never be executed due to the duplicate condition.

should_trigger: if (status == Status.Active) { Process(); } else if (status == Status.Active) { Archive(); }
if (flag == true) { DoAction1(); } else if (flag == true) { DoAction2(); }
should_not_trigger: if (status == Status.Active) { Process(); } else if (status == Status.Pending) { Queue(); }
